spec_match.rationale: Mocks a music-player whose AudioContext is paused by an OS interruption (simulated VoIP call) and auto-resumes when focus returns â€” that is the explicit user-facing scenario the spec exists for.

interactive.rationale: Real buttons + checkbox drive a running AudioContext; the event log + paused-by-system overlay update live as state transitions happen, and the page distinguishes `interrupted` from `suspended` with separate UI.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature index promises `distinguishes interrupted (OS pulled focus â€” paused by system) from suspended (user paused), with optional auto-resume strategy`. The two-state-source distinction is exactly what the page renders.

edge_cases.rationale: Multiple sections including the explicit `interrupted vs suspended â€” same surface, different UX` block, plus the auto-resume opt-out toggle. Strong coverage of the why-this-matters paths.

references.rationale: `see also` cites https://chromestatus.com/feature/5172068166139904, the audio-session spec, and the Web Audio API interrupted state. Three solid references.

design_system.rationale: 75 CSS variable references, no raw hex detected, no button-color/background mismatch. Design-system clean.

openQuestions.title: Add a real audio source instead of a mock
openQuestions.detail: The page mocks a music player but does not actually play audio. A 220 Hz tone or a short looping `decodeAudioData` buffer would let visitors hear the interruption in real time â€” sound is the whole point of the feature.

openQuestions.title: Surface the resumeOnFocus opt-out failure case
openQuestions.detail: If the auto-resume strategy is off and the system releases focus, the player should stay paused with a `tap to resume` affordance. Walk the user through that branch explicitly so they see both the auto-resume and the manual-resume paths.

openQuestions.title: Cover OS-level vs same-page interruption sources
openQuestions.detail: Interruptions can come from (a) another tab/process taking exclusive audio, (b) lid close / device sleep, (c) explicit `navigator.audioSession.type` changes. Annotate the simulator with which one each button represents so readers can map the demo onto their app's real triggers.

summary: Best-in-class concept page for this feature: explicitly disambiguates the `interrupted` vs `suspended` UX trap, ships strong references, and follows the design system. Only meaningful gap is that the player is silent â€” wiring a real oscillator would let users actually hear the pause.
